action.reasoning: Observation: Baseline eval returned macro_f1=0.029 after 2 epochs of CLIP ViT-B/16. This is far below published WILDS baselines (~0.30+), indicating severe underfitting and a weak training recipe. Analysis: 2 epochs is insufficient; augmentation is minimal (only crop+flip); no LR schedule, no AMP, no label smoothing, and class weighting uses sqrt which may still under-weight rare classes. Also, test macro_f1 is much lower than val (0.10), suggesting poor generalization to unseen locations. Decision: Switch to the stronger CLIP ViT-L/14 backbone, increase epochs to 10, add RandAugment + ColorJitter, use cosine LR with warmup, mixed precision, gradient clipping, label smoothing, and inverse-frequency class weighting. Also add test-time augmentation (TTA) in predict.py to boost OOD robustness. Measure improvement with eval.
